Where to Blitz the Gap

Pick what counts as impact and how much time you have — the map finds the best spot you can reach and get back from.

Life group & goali
Where the scores come from. Real iNaturalist observations in B.C., binned to ~25 km cells.
  • “All biodiversity” averages 7 life groups (amphibians, birds, fungi, insects, mammals, plants, reptiles).
  • Each group is a recent sample of iNat records, not the full archive — plants & fungi are capped (~10k each), so their maps are coarser.
  • Priority is backtested: holding back later-2025 sightings, higher-priority cells tended to turn up more new species.
  • At-risk & location-obscured species are left out of suggestions — we don't point people at them.
A planning aid, not ground truth — please obscure sensitive species and respect Indigenous data sovereignty. How Blitz the Gap works →

More options
Worth the drive
Drops mostly-driving trips. Round trip = there and back (both legs counted). Default: field time ≥ half your round-trip drive; pick "Any" for long hauls.
Rank by impact per kg of travel CO₂.
🔭 Plan a trip — the destination shows what you might record there.
Map style
Vector basemap — toggle layers like Maputnik. Other styles are raster (roads baked in).

How impact is scored & data sources
skip
go here
Each cell's impact 0–100 is your goal mix, relative to the best cell shown. Hover a cell to see which goals drive it.

How it works: Blitz the Gap is a B.C.-wide bioblitz — head to a high-priority spot, record what you see on iNaturalist, and your research-grade sightings flow into the 2026 project, filling the map's gaps.

Scores from real iNaturalist observations (BC), tested by holding back later sightings. Drive/cycle/walk routes from OSRM (FOSSGIS); climate from CHELSA; forest loss from Hansen GFC. Driving CO₂ ≈ 0.18 kg/km; cycling/walking zero. A planning aid — obscure sensitive-species locations and respect Indigenous data-sovereignty before any public release.
